criteria: Create and map a POSIX shared memory region accessible by multiple processes using shm_open() and mmap() with MAP_SHARED
Implement lock-free SPSC ring buffer with head/tail indices updated by separate processes
Place memory barriers correctly: release fence after data write before head update, acquire fence after head read before data read
Handle wraparound using power-of-2 buffer size with bitwise AND masking instead of modulo division
Align head and tail to separate 64-byte cache lines using alignas(64) to prevent false sharing
Implement producer backoff strategy combining spin loop with yield for CPU efficiency
Calculate and report latency statistics (min, max, median, p99, p999) from round-trip benchmark
Achieve sub-microsecond median latency in benchmark under sustained load
Handle process crash scenarios with either sequence number validation or heartbeat detection
Validate buffer size is power-of-2 at initialization time with clear error message
Flat buffer format specification with inline scalar fields and offset-based references for variable-length data (strings, vectors, nested tables)
VTable-based schema metadata enabling O(1) field access via pointer arithmetic without parsing
Schema definition language supporting tables, enums, scalars, strings, vectors, and nested types
Code generator producing type-safe C++ accessor classes (View) and builder classes with hardcoded offsets
Nested message support via 32-bit unsigned offsets allowing arbitrary graph structures within a single buffer
Vector/array support with length-prefixed contiguous storage for SIMD-friendly access patterns
Schema evolution via field IDs with forward compatibility (old readers ignore new fields) and backward compatibility (new readers use defaults for missing fields)
Alignment calculator enforcing hardware alignment constraints (8-byte for double/int64, 4-byte for int32/float, etc.) with automatic padding
Endianness handling strategy with native-endian assumption for same-machine IPC and optional byte-swapping for cross-platform
Message header format with magic number, version, type ID, and payload size for ring buffer integration
Zero-copy integration with SPSC ring buffer: serialize directly to slot, access via View over slot memory
Benchmark demonstrating at least 10x lower latency than JSON parsing for field access (target: <50ns vs >500ns)
Default value support in schema and accessor code for handling missing fields in evolved schemas
Field presence checking via vtable size comparison and offset validation
MPMC ring buffer implemented using Vyukov sequence number algorithm with per-slot atomic sequence tracking
Producers use CAS-based slot claiming with exponential backoff when contending for tail position
Consumers use CAS-based slot claiming with exponential backoff when contending for head position
Sequence numbers prevent ABA problem by monotonically increasing across ring buffer cycles
Memory barriers correctly placed with acquire semantics on loads and release semantics on stores
False sharing prevented through cache-line alignment (64 bytes) of head and tail indices
Fair scheduling implemented via round-robin shard selection for producers and consumers
Backpressure mechanism blocks producers when queue is full with configurable spin/yield/sleep thresholds
Sharded MPMC queue alternative provided for high-contention scenarios (8+ producers)
Credit-based flow control available as optional backpressure strategy
No message loss under normal operation verified through multi-producer stress tests
Performance degrades gracefully under contention (documented throughput/latency at 2/4/8 producers)
CAS failure rate metrics exposed for production monitoring
Benchmarks demonstrate latency percentiles (p50/p99/p999) at various contention levels
Correctness tests cover ABA scenario, consumer starvation, and backpressure activation
